architecture_fit.note: Single attention block, no MLP, no causal mask — a clean minimal delta from base_model.py that genuinely implements reversal (query i -> key L-1-i).

baseline_comparison.note: Relies on benchmark's built-in identity (~0.06) and random (0.0625) baselines vs 1.0; causal/ablation strawman is described but not actually run.

faithfulness.note: Purely synthetic hand-set circuit; honestly flagged, and a concrete ablation (phi(i) vs phi(L-1-i)) is proposed but not executed/shown as an artefact.

operating_range.note: Perfect acc and mirror mass across 8/16/32/64 (4x beyond canonical); construction is parametric in L so it never breaks, but no test pushes to a failure edge.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entire mechanism is hand-set on the GPU with zero learned weights — strong evidence of mechanistic understanding.

visual_judgement.note: Clean attention heatmap with mirror anti-diagonal overlay, length dropdown and beta slider, plus acc/mass and a sample input/pred/reverse — single claim is immediately legible.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties the anti-diagonal heatmap, axes, length dropdown (generalisation) and temperature slider (robustness) directly to the goal's behaviour+mechanism claims.

notes: A precise hand-built reversal head: one attention block with a Fourier delta-kernel encoding gives accuracy 1.0 and mirror mass ~1.0 at every length, with headline robustness 1.0 — exactly isolating the positional algorithm from a length-16 lookup. Architecture fit and the hardcoded-weights bonus are excellent and the visualisation is well-justified. It loses ground on faithfulness/baseline because the contrasting strawman and ablation are described rather than executed, which is the main gap for a synthetic attempt.
